note: Verification method: pulled feed URLs from each outlet's own RSS index page (Aristegui, DOF), a maintained OPML directory (spians/awesome-RSS-feeds, feedspot), and outlet arc/outboundfeeds patterns; confirmed structure where fetchable. NOTE ON BOT BLOCKS: several live WordPress/arc feeds (Animal Político /feed/, El País feeds.elpais.com, Reforma) returned 403/404 to the WebFetch bot but are confirmed real and active via the directories and their own RSS pages — they will work from the pipeline with a normal User-Agent. Flag these for a UA header + retry in the connector. WIRE COVERAGE: Reuters and AP no longer expose clean per-country RSS (Reuters retired public feeds ~2020); Mexico wire coverage is already handled by the existing GDELT build-news.js allowlist — keep sourcing AP/Reuters/AFP through GDELT rather than direct RSS, and use the outlets below as the native-Spanish politics layer. ARCHITECTURE FIT: DOF sumario.xml is the one PRIMARY-SOURCE feed here (decrees/reforms/appointments as-published) — treat it as a separate 'official' lane feeding Politics & Policy and What-to-Watch, distinct from press coverage. Recommend keying items by canonical URL (strip query/UTM) + normalized title hash for dedup across outlets covering the same event; score on significance 0-10 as Fable specified, boosting items corroborated by 2+ tier-1/tier-2 outlets or backed by a DOF entry. Range delivered: 11 sources (mix of tier-1 quality dailies, watchdog/investigative, and the official gazette).

sources.name: Aristegui Noticias — México
sources.covers: National politics, Sheinbaum/Morena, Congress, corruption investigations, security; independent watchdog voice (Carmen Aristegui).
sources.notes: CLEAN RSS, verified from their own /canales-rss page. Per-section feeds available (mexico, dinero-y-economia, mundo, opinion, investigaciones-especiales). Strong for accountability/investigations. Recommended anchor source.

sources.name: Animal Político
sources.covers: Politics, policy, security, human rights, fact-checking (El Sabueso), Morena/Congress/judicial reform; independent investigative.
sources.notes: WordPress /feed/ — real and active (in OPML directories) but returned 404 to bot fetch; needs a browser-like User-Agent header in the connector. Category feeds likely exist (/seccion/.../feed/). High signal for policy/watchdog.

sources.name: El Universal — Nación
sources.covers: National politics, government, Congress, security, elections; largest general daily of record.
sources.notes: Arc Publishing outbound feed (general and news variants: /arc/outboundfeeds/news/?outputtype=xml). Legacy /seccion/1671/rss.xml is dead (404) — use the arc outboundfeeds path. Broad, mostly free.

sources.name: Milenio
sources.covers: National politics, política, security, breaking government news; major national daily/TV group.
sources.notes: Milenio exposes RSS (listed in feedspot Mexican-news directory); confirm exact section path (/rss or /rss/politica.xml) at connector build time — verify the política section URL by fetching /rss index. High throughput, free.

sources.name: El País — México
sources.covers: Mexico politics with international/analytical framing; Sheinbaum, reforms, US-Mexico, rule of law.
sources.notes: feeds.elpais.com is a real, well-structured feed host but is blocked to our fetcher (needs UA + is behind CDN). Verify the exact mrss section slug from elpais.com/mexico RSS link at build. Some articles metered/paywalled — headline+dek in RSS is usable; flag full-text paywall.

sources.name: Proceso
sources.covers: Investigative politics, security, corruption, power analysis; adversarial weekly + daily web.
sources.notes: RSS index at /rss/ (confirmed in search). Strong investigative/political-accountability signal. Some content subscriber-only — treat as headline/dek source, flag partial paywall.

sources.name: Diario Oficial de la Federación (DOF)
sources.covers: OFFICIAL primary source: laws, decrees, reforms, presidential/appointment publications, regulatory changes as enacted.
sources.notes: Verified from DOF's own filtroRss.php: feeds are sumario.xml (main), indicadores.xml, sumario_licitaciones.xml, sumario_avisos.xml, sumario_vacantes.xml. Use sumario.xml as the authoritative 'what actually became law' lane — dedupe/cross-link press items against it. Gov infrastructure, no paywall.

sources.name: Excélsior
sources.covers: National politics, government, Congress, security; major national daily.
sources.notes: Clean site-wide rss.xml verified in OPML directory. Free, high volume; more establishment-leaning — good corroboration source for scoring.

sources.name: El Financiero
sources.covers: Politics-economy crossover, policy, fiscal/regulatory, Congress budget, Banxico/government; business daily (Bloomberg partner).
sources.notes: Arc outbound feed, verified in OPML. Best for the politics/economy boundary (reforms with market impact) — also feeds Economy room. Free RSS.

sources.name: Reforma
sources.covers: National politics, government accountability, Congress, security; agenda-setting daily of record.
sources.notes: HARD PAYWALL + bot protection: rss/portada.xml exists (in OPML) but the RSS index page 403'd our fetch and article bodies are subscriber-only. Usable ONLY for headlines/URLs (never summarize body from memory per Fable's rule). Flag as paywalled + scrape-fragile; keep for significance signal, not summarization.

sources.name: The Guardian — Mexico
sources.covers: English-language Mexico politics/security coverage for international framing and cross-check.
sources.notes: Clean, reliable RSS (verified in OPML). Fully free, full-text-friendly. Good English corroboration source and useful for the US-Mexico room; lower volume than Mexican dailies.

beat: Payments & Fintech (Mexico + LatAm) — Kuenta core beat

sources.name: iupana
sources.covers: Best specialist for this beat: LatAm digital banking, fintech and payments — sandboxes, neobank raises/licenses, payment rails, AML. Verified items: Baubap, Finsus, Mono, Revolut, Yape, Pix.
sources.cadence: several/week + Mon Exclusive & Fri Briefing newsletters
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid RSS 2.0 by direct fetch. On-topic, low noise. Anchor feed of the Payments & Fintech room.

sources.name: Contxto
sources.covers: LatAm startup/VC/tech with heavy fintech & payments coverage (Cobre, Palenca, PY9, ADN.vc). Good for deal-flow and funding rounds incl. Mexico.
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid RSS 2.0 (15 items). Second primary fintech engine alongside iupana.

sources.name: El CEO
sources.covers: Mexican business/markets/economy daily — peso, banking, fiscal policy, corporate. Filter to fintech/payments/banca keywords.
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid RSS 2.0 by direct fetch. Clean WordPress feed, full items. Add fintech keyword filter.

sources.name: Forbes Mexico — Dinero
sources.covers: Money/finance vertical: digital banking, fintech, markets, pensions, banking regulation.
sources.notes: WordPress section feed (/dinero/feed). Validate exact URL on first pull; other sections follow /<tag>/feed.

sources.name: El Economista — Sector Financiero
sources.covers: Leading Mexican financial daily; dedicated Sector Financiero desk (banks, fintech, CNBV/Banxico, markets).
sources.notes: FLAG: bot-walled (direct fetch blocked). RSS exists per /rss index + Feedspot; validate per-section feed path on first run with a real User-Agent.

sources.name: Expansion (Mexico)
sources.covers: Major Mexican business magazine — Economia and Empresas channels cover fintech, banking, deals.
sources.notes: Free real-time RSS on /canales-rss; grab Economia/Empresas feed links once then filter to fintech keywords.

sources.name: CNBV — Boletines de Prensa
sources.covers: Banking/securities regulator — Ley Fintech authorizations (IFPE/IFC licenses), sanctions, sector statistics.
sources.notes: No RSS. Scrape boletines list (also gob.mx/cnbv/prensa). Key by boletin number + date. Fintech license grants land here first.

sources.name: Banxico — Comunicados / Prensa
sources.covers: Central bank — payment-rails news (SPEI, CoDi, DiMo), rate decisions, remittances.
sources.notes: RSS for indicators (already wired). Press has NO RSS — extend Banxico connector to scrape comunicados + comunicados-miscelaneos for CoDi/DiMo/SPEI.

sources.name: Whitepaper.mx
sources.covers: Curated daily Mexican business brief (Monterrey) — surfaces fintech/payments (Clip, MercadoLibre, remittances).
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid Substack RSS BUT excerpts only + paid. FLAG: use as tip-off/signal feed, not full-text summarization.

sources.name: Bloomberg Linea (Mexico)
sources.covers: High-quality LatAm business/markets/fintech reporting (neobank raises, valuations, macro).
sources.notes: FLAG: no official RSS, largely paywalled → scrape-fragile. Use tag-page scrape for lead detection only. Lower priority.

sources.name: ABM — Asociacion de Bancos de Mexico
sources.covers: Banking-industry body — sector positions on payments, fraud, digital banking, regulation.
sources.notes: No RSS; low-volume press page, cheap to scrape. Secondary/optional.

note: Verified 2026-07-10 via live fetch. CONFIRMED valid RSS 2.0 by direct fetch: iupana, Contxto, El CEO, Whitepaper.mx. Others exist but not byte-fetched (bot walls / WordPress convention), flagged per source. Tiering: iupana + Contxto = primary fintech engines (clean RSS, on-topic). El CEO + Forbes Dinero + El Economista + Expansion = MX general-business layer (filter to fintech/payments keywords). CNBV + Banxico + ABM = regulator/primary layer, no clean RSS, need lightweight HTML scrape keyed by boletin number/date; extend the existing Banxico stats connector to also scrape its comunicados/prensa (SPEI/CoDi/DiMo). NEOBANKS: none of Nu/Klar/Stori/Uala/Mercado Pago/Spin-OXXO expose a clean per-market news RSS; best direct access is corporate IR/press for Nubank and Mercado Libre/Mercado Pago. Do NOT build per-neobank connectors — cover them via iupana/Contxto/El CEO which report raises/licenses first.

Verification method: fetched candidate feeds directly and inspected the XML. CONFIRMED live (valid RSS 2.0, fresh items dated Jul 7-10 2026): El Financiero (empresas/mercados/economia), Expansion (empresas), El CEO (/feed/). Forbes Mexico WordPress feed exists but returned 403 to the fetcher (UA/bot block) — real feed, will work from a server with a normal User-Agent header. El Economista domain refused the fetch tool entirely (bot protection) — treat as scrape-fragile / test from the pipeline runner. Bloomberg Linea runs on Arc XP so it follows the documented /arc/outboundfeeds/rss/ pattern (same engine as El Financiero, whose feeds I confirmed) but its content is largely paywalled — use for headline signal only. KEY ARCHITECTURE POINT: Reuters and Bloomberg (terminal) no longer publish free public RSS — do NOT try to add them as direct feeds; keep pulling their Mexico coverage through the existing GDELT allowlist builder (add reuters.com and bloomberg.com/bloomberglinea.com as allowed domains). For primary-source corporate filings (the highest-trust, lowest-noise signal for this beat: earnings, M&A disclosures, exec changes, IPO/listing notices), scrape BMV Eventos Relevantes and BIVA — neither exposes RSS/API, but the Eventos Relevantes list page is a stable scrape target and is the authoritative source that outlets themselves report from. Recommended beat stack: primary layer = BMV/BIVA filings (scrape) + Banxico (already have); news layer = El Financiero + Expansion + El CEO clean RSS as the spine, El Economista + Forbes as secondary, Bloomberg Linea + Reuters via GDELT for wire/exclusives. Dedup note: the same M&A/exec story will appear across El Financiero/Expansion/Reuters within hours — key items by normalized title + entity + day and cluster, don't just dedup by URL.

sources.name: El Financiero — Empresas
sources.covers: Big Mexican companies (Femsa/OXXO, América Móvil, Cemex, Bimbo, Walmex), earnings, M&A, executive moves — the core corporate beat
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid RSS 2.0, fresh items dated Jul 2026. Arc XP engine; also exposes site-wide /arc/outboundfeeds/rss/?outputType=xml. Cleanest, most reliable feed for this beat — make it the spine.

sources.name: El Financiero — Mercados
sources.covers: BMV/peso/rates market moves, BMV index, stock-specific market news, IPO pricing coverage
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid feed (sibling of Empresas, same Arc XP setup). Pair with Empresas; some overlap — dedup across the two.

sources.name: El Financiero — Economía
sources.covers: Macro/economy context around corporate news; overlaps the Economy room more than Companies
sources.notes: CONFIRMED pattern (same feed family). Optional — include if you want macro tie-ins; can be routed to the Economy room instead of Companies.

sources.name: Expansión — Empresas
sources.covers: Corporate strategy, ownership, industry deep-dives, big-company news; strong on business features and deals
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid RSS 2.0, feed title 'Expansión - Empresas', items dated Jul 7-10 2026. Section RSS pattern is /rss/{seccion} (also /rss/economia, /rss/mercados). High-quality business desk; second pillar of the spine.

sources.name: El CEO
sources.covers: Mexican business/finance dailies — companies, markets, banking, executive moves, fintech; business-only outlet
sources.notes: CONFIRMED valid WordPress RSS (/feed/). Business-native outlet, good signal-to-noise for this exact beat. Category feeds available via /categoria/{slug}/feed/ if you want to narrow to empresas/mercados.

sources.name: El Economista
sources.covers: Markets, empresas, finanzas, sectores — one of the two leading MX financial dailies alongside El Financiero
sources.notes: SCRAPE-FRAGILE: domain refused the fetch tool (bot protection), so I could not confirm the live feed URL. Historical feed pattern is /rss/{seccion} (mercados, finanzas, empresas). Test the endpoint from the GitHub Actions runner with a normal User-Agent; if RSS 403s, fall back to HTML scrape of section pages. High-value outlet — worth the extra handling.

sources.name: Forbes México
sources.covers: Companies, billionaires/executives, deals, IPOs, business features; strong on exec-move and wealth angles
sources.notes: WordPress /feed/ exists but returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool (UA/bot block). Feed is real; set a browser-like User-Agent from the runner. Category feeds via /tag/{slug}/feed/. Secondary source; good for exec-move stories.

sources.name: Bloomberg Línea México
sources.covers: Markets, deals, IPOs, macro, exec moves with a finance/investor lens; Latam-wide with MX section
sources.notes: PAYWALL + VERIFY: /feed/ 404s; runs on Arc XP so the working feed follows /arc/outboundfeeds/rss/ (same engine as El Financiero, which I confirmed). Most article bodies are paywalled — use for headline/lead signal only, do not rely on full-text summarization. Also reachable via GDELT allowlist.

sources.name: BMV — Eventos Relevantes (issuer filings)
sources.endpoint: no RSS/API — scrape the Eventos Relevantes listing
sources.covers: PRIMARY SOURCE: material disclosures by listed issuers — M&A, earnings, board/exec changes, buybacks, IPO/listing notices. Highest-trust, zero-spin signal
sources.notes: No public RSS or documented API found. The Eventos Relevantes page is a stable scrape target and is the authoritative source outlets themselves report from — scraping it lets you break/confirm corporate news ahead of the press. Highest-value add for this beat; pair with news feeds for context.

sources.name: BIVA — Bolsa Institucional de Valores
sources.covers: Second Mexican exchange: listings, IPOs on BIVA, issuer notices; complements BMV for full-market IPO/filing coverage
sources.notes: No RSS/API. Lower volume than BMV but catches IPOs/listings that debut on BIVA. Scrape-fragile; optional second primary source — add only if BMV alone misses BIVA-listed names.

sources.name: Reuters (Mexico coverage)
sources.endpoint: no free public RSS — pull via GDELT allowlist (domain reuters.com)
sources.covers: Wire-grade breaking on major MX corporate M&A, earnings, exec moves, IPOs; English, often first
sources.notes: Reuters retired free public RSS; direct feeds are dead and the official API is paid/enterprise. DO NOT add as a direct feed — you already have the GDELT builder; just ensure reuters.com is on the allowlist. Highest trust tier for verification/cross-check.

sources.name: Mexico Business News
sources.covers: English-language MX industry/business coverage — companies, deals, sector analysis; useful for an English brief and cross-checking
sources.notes: Listed among Mexico business RSS feeds by aggregators; confirm exact endpoint from the runner. English-language option if the brief wants an en source or a second confirmation on deals. Lower priority than the Spanish spine.

note: Verification pass done 2026-07-10 via WebSearch/WebFetch. Key structural finding for this beat: the highest-trust wires (Reuters, Bloomberg) and DC-trade specialists (Politico Pro, Inside U.S. Trade) have NO usable public RSS — Reuters killed feeds.reuters.com in 2020, Bloomberg's are deprecated/paywalled, Inside U.S. Trade is subscription-only. So this beat splits into two clean layers: (a) PRIMARY/OFFICIAL sources you fetch directly and can trust verbatim — the Federal Register API (already wired; just add USTR + CBP agency filters) and CBP's GovDelivery RSS; and (b) a WIRE/AGGREGATOR layer where a Google News RSS query (verified live, returns dated Reuters/AP/Politico/El Pais items) is the pragmatic, allowlist-friendly substitute for the paywalled outlets — dedupe it against the existing GDELT builder, which already covers the same wires. Recommendation: lean on the two official APIs as the spine of this beat (they are the actual news — tariff actions, USMCA review notices, worker-rights cases), and treat wires as context. Reuters/Bloomberg/Inside-Trade should stay on the allowlist as trusted DOMAINS surfaced via GDELT/Google News, not as direct fetches. What we already wire: US Federal Register (generic) — this beat just needs the agency-scoped queries below. USTR is on the Federal Register (agency slug 'trade-representative-office-of-united-states'), so USTR tariff/Section-301 notices come free through the wire we already run.

sources.name: Federal Register API — USTR notices
sources.covers: USTR tariff actions, Section 301 investigations, USMCA-related Federal Register notices, comment/hearing dockets
sources.cadence: Publishes on business days; poll daily
sources.notes: VERIFIED live — returns clean JSON, no API key. Extends the Federal Register connector we ALREADY wire; just add the agency filter. This is the authoritative source for USTR tariff/301 actions since USTR itself has no RSS. Highest signal on this beat.

sources.name: Federal Register API — CBP notices
sources.covers: CBP import rules, tariff-rate quotas, forced-labor/UFLPA determinations, border-processing rules affecting cross-border trade
sources.notes: Same already-wired Federal Register API, different agency slug. Official primary source. No key. Pairs with CBP RSS below (RSS = press-release framing, API = rulemaking).

sources.name: CBP Newsroom (media releases)
sources.covers: Border security, immigration/CBP enforcement, seizures, port-of-entry and trade-facilitation announcements
sources.notes: GovDelivery-backed RSS — VALID feed, but returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (bot filter). Will work from the Node pipeline with a real User-Agent header; if it keeps 403-ing, fall back to scraping /newsroom/media-releases/all. Sub-feeds exist: /rss/newsroom/media-releases (trade & border rolled up). Primary source for the immigration/border slice of this beat.

sources.name: USTR Press Office
sources.covers: USMCA joint-review readouts, US-Mexico ministerial statements, worker-rights (RRM) cases, trade-negotiation announcements
sources.notes: VERIFIED the page carries exactly this beat's news (e.g. 'US and Mexico Conclude First Bilateral Round of USMCA Joint Review' May 2026; Greer–Ebrard joint statement Jun 2026). NO RSS feed exposed — must scrape the listing HTML (stable structure). Highest-value narrative source for USMCA review; complements the FR API which only carries formal notices.

sources.name: Google News RSS — USMCA/tariffs query
sources.covers: Cross-border wire/analysis headlines: Reuters, AP, Politico, El Pais, Automotive News, PIIE, Mexico Business News
sources.notes: VERIFIED live — returns 50+ dated items incl. Reuters/AP/Politico. This is the practical stand-in for Reuters/Bloomberg/Politico whose own RSS is dead or paywalled. MUST be filtered against your trusted-source allowlist (same discipline as build-news.js) and DEDUPED against GDELT, which already covers these wires — otherwise double-counting. Summarize only fetched text, never the Google snippet.

sources.name: Politico — Trade / Economy
sources.endpoint: https://www.politico.com/rss/economy.xml (section RSS; verify slug from politico.com/rss index)
sources.covers: Washington trade-policy politics: USMCA review politics, tariff legislation, USTR/Congress dynamics
sources.notes: Politico blocks direct WebFetch (bot filter) so couldn't confirm the exact section-feed URL live; RSS index is documented at politico.com/rss with per-section XML feeds. FLAG: the deep trade coverage ('Morning Trade') is Politico Pro — PAYWALLED. Use the free trade/economy section RSS only; expect headlines + lede, not full text. Reach the rest via Google News/GDELT.

sources.name: Inside U.S. Trade
sources.covers: Deep specialist US trade-policy reporting: USMCA review mechanics, tariff dockets, negotiation blow-by-blow
sources.notes: FLAG: subscription/paywalled (Inside Washington Publishers), NO RSS. Best-in-class specialist but you can only pull public headline/teaser text — never full articles. Keep the DOMAIN on the allowlist so its stories surface via Google News/GDELT; do not build a direct-fetch connector. Scrape-fragile + paywall = low priority to wire.

sources.name: El Financiero — Economía
sources.covers: Mexican-side trade-desk coverage: peso/trade, USMCA from Mexico City, Ebrard/Secretaría de Economía angle
sources.notes: Runs on Arc Publishing, which exposes /arc/outboundfeeds/rss/ feeds; general feed confirmed to exist though WebFetch returned truncated (Arc anti-bot). Verify the exact outputType/section query from the pipeline with a UA header. Gives the crucial MEXICO-SIDE framing this beat needs (US sources alone are one-sided). Spanish — summarizer must handle ES.

sources.name: El Economista (MX) — Comercio Exterior
sources.endpoint: scrape section (RSS not confirmed for .com.mx edition)
sources.covers: Mexican business/foreign-trade reporting: tariffs' impact on Mexican exporters, auto/steel sectors, USMCA
sources.notes: Distinct from the Spain edition eleconomista.es (which HAS RSS at /rss/). The Mexican edition's RSS wasn't confirmed — treat as scrape or reach via Google News with site: filter. Second Mexican voice for balance; lower priority than El Financiero.

sources.name: PIIE (Peterson Institute) — Trade & Investment
sources.covers: Analysis/context on USMCA economics, tariff cost modeling (not breaking news)
sources.notes: Context/explainer layer, not a news wire. Surfaced in the Google News verification ('Ending USMCA could fuel higher US consumer prices', Jul 2026). Optional — good for the 'What to Watch' room, low volume, clean RSS. Wire only if you want analytical depth.

sources.name: Banxico — Monetary policy statements & minutes
sources.covers: Central-bank rate decisions, the accompanying communiqué, and the minutes (minutas) that move the peso and set the macro tone. The single most important primary macro source.
sources.cadence: Calendar-driven: ~8 rate decisions/year (roughly every 6 weeks); minutes released ~2 weeks after each decision.
sources.notes: VERIFIED: no RSS/JSON on the announcements page — only HTML listings linking to PDFs. Scrape the listing page against the published calendar (banxico.org.mx/viewers/JSP/calendarioDifusion_en.jsp). Trigger trick: the target-rate indicator RSS (next row) fires on decision day, so use it as the signal to fetch the new statement PDF. Stable URLs, PDF body text — clean to parse.

sources.name: Banxico — Indicator RSS feeds (rate, peso, remittances)
sources.covers: Machine-readable target rate, FIX exchange rate, TIIE/CETES, remittances, reserves — the real-time data spine, and a decision-day trigger for the statements scrape.
sources.cadence: Per-publication (rate on decision days; FX daily; remittances/reserves monthly).
sources.notes: VERIFIED live RSS 2.0. Some already wired for the data board; the tasObj feed doubles as the news trigger for rate-decision items. Zero-scrape, most reliable Banxico surface.

sources.name: El Financiero — Economía
sources.covers: Mexico's leading finance/econ daily. Inflation, jobs (IMSS), trade balance, T-MEC, industrial policy, FDI rankings, energy/oil — the workhorse econ desk.
sources.cadence: Continuous; ~12 items in the economia feed, refreshed through the day.
sources.notes: VERIFIED live: pulled 12 current economia items (inflation 3.37%, IMSS fraud, T-MEC, FDI rank) on 2026-07-10. Arc XP outboundfeeds — swap category slug (mercados, empresas) for sibling feeds. Cleanest Mexican-outlet RSS found.

sources.name: Bloomberg Línea México
sources.covers: Markets/macro with strong Banxico coverage (minutes, rate path), formal-employment data, FDI, corporate finance — Bloomberg editorial standard, free tier.
sources.notes: VERIFIED live RSS 2.0; feed carried a Banxico-minutes item and Mexico employment item on 2026-07-10. Feed is pan-LatAm — filter to Mexico by URL path (/mexico/) or the tags feed to drop Argentina/Colombia noise.

sources.name: El Economista (MX)
sources.covers: The other major Mexican econ daily — macro, mercados, sector/FDI coverage; strong complement to El Financiero for cross-confirmation.
sources.notes: FLAG scrape-fragile: site returned bot-blocks (WebFetch refused) — needs a real User-Agent header and is bot-hostile. RSS exists (Arc XP) but is gated; treat as scrape-with-UA or reach it via the GDELT allowlist rather than a naive feed pull.

sources.name: Expansión — Economía
sources.endpoint: https://expansion.mx/canales-rss (RSS hub; per-section economia/empresas feeds linked there)
sources.covers: Business/economy weekly-plus-daily; nearshoring, corporates, policy, deals — good Companies & Deals crossover.
sources.notes: PARTIAL: canales-rss hub confirmed to exist and advertise free real-time feeds, but the page renders feed links client-side so the exact economia .xml URL wasn't machine-extractable. Resolve the section feed URL once from the hub, then it's a stable RSS pull.

sources.name: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas — Mexico Economic Update & research
sources.covers: Best free English-language analytical read on the Mexican macro/nearshoring story — FDI reality vs. hype, manufacturing, US-Mexico linkages. Adds analysis the wires don't.
sources.cadence: Mexico Economic Update ~8x/year (week after each FOMC); research notes irregular.
sources.notes: FLAG: RSS hub and Mexico topic pages are real but returned 403 to WebFetch (bot filter) — needs UA header/scrape fallback. Low volume, high signal; good for 'What to Watch' / context, not breaking news.

sources.name: Reuters — Mexico (via GDELT allowlist)
sources.endpoint: no public RSS since 2020 — ingest through the existing GDELT build-news allowlist (domain: reuters.com)
sources.covers: Wire-grade breaking macro: rate moves, FX, nearshoring/FDI announcements, trade/tariff developments — the tier-1 confirmation source.
sources.notes: FLAG: Reuters retired public RSS; don't build a feed connector. Already reachable via your GDELT pipeline — just ensure reuters.com is on the allowlist and let GDELT surface Mexico-economy articles. Same applies to AP/Bloomberg terminal content.

sources.name: El País — Economía / América (México)
sources.covers: Quality Spanish-language macro/politics-of-economy coverage with a dedicated Mexico edition; good for narrative and policy framing.
sources.notes: PARTIAL: El País publishes MRSS feeds (feeds.elpais.com/mrss-s/…) but the host refused WebFetch, so the economia feed wasn't fully validated live. Feed pattern is well-documented; verify the exact section slug once. Pan-Hispanic — filter to /mexico/.

sources.name: INEGI — Sala de prensa / boletines
sources.endpoint: Boletines/comunicados (HTML) + INEGI data API (BIE/BISE) already wired for the data board
sources.covers: Official releases behind the numbers: GDP (PIB), IGAE activity index, inflation (INPC), employment (ENOE), industrial production — the primary-source press release when a data print is the story.
sources.cadence: Calendar-driven per indicator (monthly/quarterly); schedule published in advance.
sources.notes: Data already flows via the INEGI API; add the boletín scrape only to caption a data release as a news item. Release calendar lets you pre-schedule the fetch.

sources.name: Secretaría de Economía / Presidencia — press (gob.mx)
sources.endpoint: https://www.gob.mx/se/prensa (HTML; gob.mx exposes /api/… JSON on some article endpoints)
sources.covers: Official nearshoring/FDI/Plan México announcements, investment commitments, trade-negotiation posture — the primary source for government-side nearshoring news.
sources.notes: FLAG scrape: gob.mx press pages are HTML; some expose JSON via /api paths but it's undocumented and inconsistent. Low volume; pairs with El Financiero/El Economista which usually report the same announcements with more context — treat gov as confirmation, not primary discovery.

sources.name: IMCO (Instituto Mexicano para la Competitividad)
sources.covers: Think-tank analysis on nearshoring/FDI, competitiveness, energy, fiscal — the analytical 'so what' on structural macro themes.
sources.notes: PARTIAL: WordPress-style site likely exposes /feed/ (unverified). Low volume, high analytical value for 'What to Watch'. Confirm the /feed/ endpoint once; otherwise light scrape of the publications index.

note: Verified live via WebFetch: El Financiero economia RSS (12 items, 2026-07-10), Bloomberg Línea RSS (Mexico items incl. Banxico minutes), Banxico indicator RSS page, and Banxico monetary-policy page confirmed to have NO RSS (scrape-only). Bot-blocked (403/refused) and needing UA/scrape fallback: El Economista MX, Dallas Fed, El País feeds host — real sources, fragile access. Key architecture takeaways for this beat: (1) Banxico is the tier-1 primary but is scrape-only for statements/minutes — use its tasObj indicator RSS as the decision-day trigger to fetch the statement PDF. (2) El Financiero + Bloomberg Línea are the two clean, verified RSS workhorses — start here. (3) El Economista as second-daily cross-confirmation but route it through the GDELT allowlist rather than a naive feed pull, since it bot-blocks. (4) Reuters has no RSS since 2020 — keep it in the GDELT allowlist path, don't build a connector. (5) Most Mexican outlets (El Financiero, Bloomberg Línea, El Economista) run Arc XP, so the same /arc/outboundfeeds/rss/ connector shape is reusable across them — one connector, many feeds. (6) Dallas Fed / IMCO are low-volume analysis for 'What to Watch', not breaking news.

beat: VC, startups & deals (Mexican funding rounds, VC activity, M&A)

sources.name: LatamList
sources.covers: The single best fit for this beat: LatAm (incl. Mexico) startup funding rounds, M&A, credit lines, and VC activity, item-by-item. Verified live 2026-07 with 10+ deal items in the prior week (e.g. 'Nexu secures $143M credit line from HSBC', 'Jota raises $30M Series A').
sources.notes: TOP PICK. Clean WordPress RSS 2.0, full content:encoded, dated pubDate. Deal-first framing = ideal for the Companies & Deals room. Filter items for Mexico by country tag/keyword since feed is pan-LatAm.

sources.name: Contxto
sources.covers: LatAm startup / VC / tech news and funding; Mexico-heavy. Verified valid RSS 2.0 (WordPress), hourly build.
sources.cadence: irregular / weekly (last items spanned weeks, not daily)
sources.notes: Real feed but publishing cadence has thinned — do not rely on it alone for weekly deal flow. Spanish edition + Substack ('Lo más destacado de la semana') also exist. Keep as secondary.

sources.name: Crunchbase News
sources.covers: Global startup funding + 'Week's 10 Biggest Rounds' + explicit LatAm coverage (e.g. 'LatAm Startup Funding Rebounds 2025, Mexico Surge'). Verified valid RSS with daily items.
sources.notes: Global feed — Mexico items are a minority, so keyword-filter for Mexico/LatAm. Authoritative on numbers/valuations. Note: /venture/feed/ and /sections/latam/feed/ both 404 — use the root /feed/ only.

sources.name: Bloomberg Línea (México)
sources.covers: Mexican + LatAm tech, startups and deals in Spanish; strong on fintech/payments rounds and corporate M&A.
sources.notes: Arc-Publishing outbound feeds. The tecnologia category feed is verified live but skews to markets/Wall Street — filter for startup/deal keywords. category/mexico feed is valid but returned an empty item set in snapshot; tag-based feeds (…/rss/tag/startup) 404. Also exposes a tags index at /arc/outboundfeeds/tags-feed.xml. Possible metered paywall on some articles.

sources.name: LAVCA (Latin American Venture Capital Assoc.)
sources.covers: Authoritative VC/PE deal data, fundraising, exits across LatAm incl. Mexico. 'Venture Bulletin' = biweekly round-up of LatAm tech transactions; 'Private Capital Update' = biweekly all-asset transactions.
sources.access: scrape (news feed page) + email newsletter (no public RSS found)
sources.notes: FLAG: no clean RSS located — access is the newsletter (subscribe) or scraping lavca.org/vc/newsfeed/. Best used as a biweekly cross-check / 'What to Watch' source, not a daily wire input.

sources.name: TechCrunch — Mexico tag
sources.covers: Occasional but high-signal Mexico startup/fintech stories (e.g. Aplazo BNPL, AWS $5B Mexico region). Verified valid RSS.
sources.notes: Low volume — treat as opportunistic. When it fires, it's usually a significant round or major-player move worth the lead.

sources.name: iupana
sources.covers: LatAm fintech / digital banking / payments deals and funding — overlaps the Payments & Fintech room but surfaces many Mexican fintech rounds relevant here.
sources.notes: Directly relevant to Alan's Kuenta beat. Confirm whether a /feed exists; otherwise newsletter or scrape. Deduplicate against LatamList/Bloomberg Línea (heavy overlap on fintech rounds).

sources.name: Forbes México — Emprendedores
sources.covers: Mexican startups, founders, and investment/expansion news in Spanish.
sources.access: RSS (feed URL follows WP convention) — scrape-fragile
sources.notes: FLAG: feed returned HTTP 403 (Cloudflare bot block) on fetch. Feed likely real but needs a real User-Agent / may resist headless fetch. Test with proper headers before relying on it; fall back to scraping the section page.

sources.name: Expansión — Emprendedores
sources.covers: Mexican entrepreneurship, startup profiles, financing analysis. Verified valid RSS (es-mx).
sources.cadence: low / evergreen (advice + analysis more than breaking deals)
sources.notes: Real, clean feed but content skews evergreen how-to over dated deal news. Useful for context/'What to Watch', weak for the deal wire itself.

note: Verified live in July 2026. Ranked best-first for a deal wire. Recommended core set for this beat: LatamList (primary, deal-by-deal RSS), Crunchbase News (numbers/valuations + biggest-rounds, filter to Mexico), Bloomberg Línea (Spanish-language Mexico coverage), plus LAVCA biweekly as an authority cross-check. Contxto/TechCrunch/Forbes/Expansión/iupana are secondary or opportunistic. Two flags: Forbes México feed is Cloudflare-blocked (403) so scrape-fragile, and LAVCA has no public RSS (newsletter/scrape only). Since most feeds are pan-LatAm, the pipeline needs a Mexico keyword/entity filter and cross-feed dedup (LatamList, Bloomberg Línea, Crunchbase, and iupana will frequently report the same Mexican fintech round).
